cross_category_overview: Across all categories, regulators are converging on heightened, cross-agency enforcement — particularly AML/CTF (AUSTRAC-Tabcorp, NDIS fraud taskforce), licensee/director accountability (ASIC, ACMA, SAMS Act), and risk-based supervisory frameworks (TEQSA, CIRMP, Aged Care) — with intensifying scrutiny of AI, cyber and digital governance creating a unified compliance uplift agenda for clients. Concurrently, a wave of new or reshaped regulators and reform regimes (ATEC, ATEC-aligned TEQSA, NDIS 2026, super switching, EPBC 'single-touch', critical infrastructure rules, digital asset framework) is opening a sustained advisory pipeline around transition readiness, governance uplift, board attestations, and program assurance across both private and Commonwealth-funded sectors.

hub_wide.brief: Three regulator investment themes define the fortnight: AML/CTF and financial crime enforcement is escalating in lockstep across AUSTRAC (Tabcorp probe, ~$500m market-cap hit) and the AFP-led Fraud Fusion Taskforce; ASIC is industrialising surveillance and director accountability (Canva $792k infringements, Berndale criminal plea, ABL AFSL cancellation, Hilellis disqualification); and AI/regtech is now a regulator capability play in its own right — CASA's AI transparency statement and myCASA channel, Melbourne Airport's emergency-ops AI agents, and state AI safety cameras under fairness challenge. Sector regulators are simultaneously rebuilding the rulebook: AER is finalising CitiPower/Powercor 2026-31 revenue and Transgrid EnergyConnect reopener, Home Affairs has reopened CIRMP Rules consultation, the Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission is operationalising the rights-based Act, and ATEC has formally stood up under David Turvey PSM. Named clients in the spotlight — Tabcorp, Canva, CBA (vs Apple), Australian Radio Network, SpinTel, CitiPower/Powercor, Jemena, Transgrid, Melbourne Airport and ANU — are all live BD conversations this fortnight. KPMG should concentrate BD effort on three pursuit lanes: an AML/CTF program assurance and financial-crime tech-uplift play targeting wagering and non-bank reporting entities before AUSTRAC moves to enforceable undertakings; a CIRMP + AI governance bundle for aviation, road and port operators anchored to CASA's AI posture; and a combined AFSL governance, financial-reporting assurance and AI cyber-resilience proposition for mid-tier funds managers and advice licensees facing the 2026 super switching deadline. Aged Care + NDIS 2026 readiness and an ATEC stand-up advisory play (PMO, TOM, data spine) round out the priority pursuit map. Move now — the enforcement and rulemaking windows close fast.

Advisory opportunities cluster around gambling licensing/AML uplift, tobacco excise enforcement support, procurement probity reviews, and pastoral/agribusiness ESG and reporting compliance.
